WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
The Capesize Timecharter Average (5TC) slipped by $534 from last Friday, closing at $29,518 amid declines across both basins. Despite the drop, the BCI 5TC remains more than $20,000 above the level recorded at the same time last year. Activity was muted with only one miner reported in the Pacific. Rio Tinto was rumoured to have fixed at mid $9s for 170,000/10 Dampier to Qingdao but details remained unconfirmed. In the Atlantic, discussions on vessels and cargoes were limited. From Brazil, C3 showed marginal improvement, settling at $24.214 with market chatter focusing on cargoes with early February laycan. Last Friday, Wansa was linked to taking the Genius (175,580 2012) for 170,000/10 Tubarao to Qingdao on 17/22 January at $26. No fresh C3 fixtures had been concluded at the time of writing.
PANAMAX
The week opened quietly, with the seasonal wind-down toward Christmas clearly underway and overall activity thinning across both the Atlantic and Pacific basins. Market sentiment continued to drift lower amid a persistent lack of fresh fixture activity. Trans-Atlantic and fronthaul rates remained under pressure as charterers tested softer levels, with owner resistance gradually easing. In Asia, conditions weakened further, with limited cargo availability and little in the way of replenishment keeping activity subdued. An oversupply of prompt tonnage continued to weigh on fundamentals, enabling charterers to circulate increasingly aggressive rate ideas for early cover. Period activity remained slim with only the Myrsini (82,117 2010) open in Haldia 31 December/1 January reported as fixed for 12/14 months with redelivery worldwide at $13,500 to Messrs Paralos. The P5TC average fell by $368 to close at $11,540.
Atlantic
The Alexandria (82,852 2012) open in Stade 25/30 December fixed a trip via US East Coast redelivery Poland at $12,000 to Cargill.
Asia
The Legato II (78,245 2013) fixed basis retro sailing from Nihama 18 December for a trip via Indonesia redelivery South China at $6,000 although a charterer was not disclosed.
SUPRAMAX
With the holiday season upon us, unsurprisingly it was a rather lacklustre start to the week. The Atlantic lost further ground, in the US Gulf the lack of fresh enquiry and a build up of prompt tonnage saw rates drop further. Also, both the South Atlantic and Mediterranean- Continent lacked fresh impetus. From Asia, again further negative sentiment with limited fresh opportunities open for owners and charterers remaining in the driving seat. A little action appeared from the Indian Ocean, although again rates remained under downward pressure. Period activity remained slow, although it surfaced that the Darya Priya (64,760 2025) Tianjin 1 January was fixed for short period, but no more details came to light. The 11TC average finished the day down $358 at $15,038.
Atlantic
The BBG Leader (63,241 2015) was heard fixed basis delivery EC South America for a trip redelivery Far East at $16,500 plus $650,000 ballast bonus to HSSC.
Asia
The Meghna Progress (66,140 2023) was heard basis delivery Porbandar or Pipavav for a trip via Oman to Ennore or Kattupali with gypsum at $16,250 to Team Bulk. The Rising Sky.Gr (63,405 2017) was also heard fixed delivery Salalah redelivery Durban with gypsum at $12,000. The Kallone (55,840 2007) was also heard to have been fixed basis delivery Richards Bay for a trip Arabian Gulf/WC India with coal at $14,000 + $140,000 ballast bonus.
HANDYSIZE
As expected, a quiet start to the week was seen as the festive season approaches, with negativity across both basins continuing to dominate. The BHSI fell a further 9 points to 737, while the 7TC closed the day at $13,261. In the Continent and Mediterranean, rates continued to decline, particularly in the East and West Mediterranean, due to a lack of fresh demand. Similarly, the South Atlantic and US Gulf experienced fewer enquiries, contributing to an overall weaker tone. In Asia, conditions remain equally challenging, with limited fresh cargoes and very few prompt or spot fixtures being reported. Owners are increasingly finding themselves having to reduce rate expectations in order to secure employment. The BBC Mercury (39,869 2023) open Busan heard fixed steels from China to Vietnam in the $8,000s, but further details were not disclosed. On the period front, the Sea Topaz (32,484 2010) was reported fixed for delivery EC India - Indonesia–Taiwan range for a period of minimum 3 to about 5 months at $13,500 by LTE, though further details were not disclosed.
All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
Thanks and best regards.
